revision.text:
{{Nihongo|'''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858-887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]]<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref>. Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
==Final National History==
''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, ''[[Shinkokushi]]'', was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''.
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References==
* {{cite book | last = Kubota | first = Jun | title = Iwanami Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | date = 2007 | language = Japanese | id = ISBN 978-4-00-080310-6 }}
{{Japan-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}
[[Category:Late Old Japanese texts]] [[Category:History books]]
[[ja:日本三代実録]]

{{Nihongo|'''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858-887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is an earthquake in July 869 and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [http://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times, web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, ''[[Shinkokushi]]'', was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''.
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * {{NKBJ}}

{{Rikkokushi}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}

{{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>

{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.

{{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>

A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.

{{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

{{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

{{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

{{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

{{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

{{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

{{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

{{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.
==Notes== {{Reflist}}

{{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

{{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

{{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * {{NKBJ}}
== External links == *[https://web.archive.org/web/20180519144319/http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/sandai.html Text of the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''] (Japanese)
{{Rikkokushi}} {{Japan-hist-stub}}

{{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.

Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.

* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

{{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[:en:History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[:en:Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[:en:Ariwara no Narihira|Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[:en:869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>

In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Sandai Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fa:نیهون ساندای جیتسوروکو]][[ff:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon sandai jitsuroku]][[it:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ja:日本三代実録]][[ko:일본삼대실록]][[pt:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ru:Нихон сандай дзицуроку]][[zh_classical:日本三代實錄]][[zh:日本三代實錄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1052981]] Wikidata name: Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[:en:History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[:en:Kanbun|Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[:en:Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[:en:Ariwara no Narihira|Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[:en:869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

The text was completed in 901.

Written in [[:en:Kanbun|Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[:en:Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[:en:Ariwara no Narihira|Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[:en:869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>

In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Sandai Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fa:نیهون ساندای جیتسوروکو]][[ff:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon sandai jitsuroku]][[it:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ja:日本三代実録]][[ko:일본삼대실록]][[pt:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ru:Нихон сандай дзицуроку]][[zh-classical:日本三代實錄]][[zh:日本三代實錄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1052981]] Wikidata name: Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[:en:History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[:en:Kanbun|Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[:en:Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[:en:Ariwara no Narihira|Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[:en:869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

[[en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]
[[Category:Pages without translation templates]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} [[cs:Nihon Sandai Džicuroku]] [[de:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] [[es:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] [[fa:نیهون ساندای جیتسوروکو]] [[fr:Nihon sandai jitsuroku]] [[it:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] [[ja:日本三代実録]] [[ko:일본삼대실록]] [[pt:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] [[ru:Нихон сандай дзицуроку]] [[zh:日本三代實錄]] [[zh-classical:日本三代實錄]] {{wikidata link|Q1052981}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Sandai Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fa:نیهون ساندای جیتسوروکو]][[ff:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon sandai jitsuroku]][[it:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ja:日本三代実録]][[ko:일본삼대실록]][[pt:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ru:Нихон сандай дзицуроку]][[zh-classical:日本三代實錄]][[zh:日本三代實錄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1052981]] Wikidata name: Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[:en:History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[:en:Kanbun|Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[:en:Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[:en:Ariwara no Narihira|Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[:en:869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

Also described is [[:en:869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Sandai Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fa:نیهون ساندای جیتسوروکو]][[ff:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon sandai jitsuroku]][[it:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ja:日本三代実録]][[ko:일본삼대실록]][[pt:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ru:Нихон сандай дзицуроку]][[zh_classical:日本三代實錄]][[zh:日本三代實錄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Sandai Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fa:نیهون ساندای جیتسوروکو]][[ff:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon sandai jitsuroku]][[it:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ja:日本三代実録]][[ko:일본삼대실록]][[pt:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ru:Нихон сандай дзицуроку]][[zh_classical:日本三代實錄]][[zh:日本三代實錄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Sandai Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fa:نیهون ساندای جیتسوروکو]][[ff:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon sandai jitsuroku]][[it:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ja:日本三代実録]][[ko:일본삼대실록]][[pt:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ru:Нихон сандай дзицуроку]][[zh-classical:日本三代實錄]][[zh:日本三代實錄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1052981]] Wikidata name: Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[:en:History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[:en:Kanbun|Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[:en:Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[:en:Ariwara no Narihira|Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[:en:869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Sandai Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fa:نیهون ساندای جیتسوروکو]][[ff:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon sandai jitsuroku]][[it:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ja:日本三代実録]][[ko:일본삼대실록]][[pt:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ru:Нихон сандай дзицуроку]][[zh_classical:日本三代實錄]][[zh:日本三代實錄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Sandai Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fa:نیهون ساندای جیتسوروکو]][[ff:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon sandai jitsuroku]][[it:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ja:日本三代実録]][[ko:일본삼대실록]][[pt:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ru:Нихон сандай дзицуроку]][[zh_classical:日本三代實錄]][[zh:日本三代實錄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Sandai Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fa:نیهون ساندای جیتسوروکو]][[ff:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon sandai jitsuroku]][[it:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ja:日本三代実録]][[ko:일본삼대실록]][[pt:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]][[ru:Нихон сандай дзицуроку]][[zh-classical:日本三代實錄]][[zh:日本三代實錄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1052981]] Wikidata name: Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[:en:History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by [[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]], [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in [[:en:Kanbun|Kanbun]]-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Seiwa]], [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Yōzei]], and [[:en:Emperor Kōkō|Kōkō]]. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of [[:en:Ariwara no Narihira|Ariwara no Narihira]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[:en:869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda}} ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|en|Fujiwara no Tokihira|WD=Q2994735|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}, [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in {{ill|Kanbun|en|Kanbun|WD=Q7901319|lt=Kanbun}}-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|en|Emperor Seiwa|WD=Q349243|lt=Seiwa}}, {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|en|Emperor Yōzei|WD=Q349280|lt=Yōzei}}, and {{ill|Emperor Kōkō|en|Emperor Kōkō|WD=Q440518|lt=Kōkō}}. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|en|Ariwara no Narihira|WD=Q451991|lt=Ariwara no Narihira}}.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[:en:869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q36204|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}, according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.

{{Short description|Japanese history text of 901}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'''''|日本三代実録||"The True History of Three Reigns of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Sandai Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned {{ill|History of Japan|en|History of Japan|WD=Q130436|lt=Japanese history}} text. Completed in 901, it is the sixth and final text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 858–887.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]'' (879), {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda}} ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was compiled by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|en|Fujiwara no Tokihira|WD=Q2994735|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}, [[Ōkura no Yoshiyuki]], and [[Mimune no Masahira]]. The text was completed in 901.
==Contents==
Written in {{ill|Kanbun|en|Kanbun|WD=Q7901319|lt=Kanbun}}-style and contained within fifty volumes, the contents cover a span of thirty years from 858 to 887 corresponding to three imperial reigns: {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|en|Emperor Seiwa|WD=Q349243|lt=Seiwa}}, {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|en|Emperor Yōzei|WD=Q349280|lt=Yōzei}}, and {{ill|Emperor Kōkō|en|Emperor Kōkō|WD=Q440518|lt=Kōkō}}. It contains many imperial edicts and is more detailed compared to the earlier texts. Particularly famous is a description of {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|en|Ariwara no Narihira|WD=Q451991|lt=Ariwara no Narihira}}.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:155)</ref> Parts of volumes 15, 19, and 48 are incomplete.
Also described is [[:en:869 Jogan Sanriku earthquake|an earthquake in July 869]] and a tsunami that flooded the plains of northeast Japan: “The sea soon rushed into the villages and towns, overwhelming a few hundred miles of land along the coast. There was scarcely any time for escape, though there were boats and the high ground just before them. In this way about 1,000 people were killed.” These were the same plains that were submerged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q36204|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}, according to one account. "Analysis of sediments left by the 869 tsunami led to an estimate that the earthquake had a magnitude of 8.3."<ref>Chang, Kenneth, [https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/22/science/22predict.html?pagewanted=2&hp "Blindsided by Ferocity Unleashed by a Fault"], ''The New York Times'', web p. 2, March 21, 2011 (March 22, 2011 p. D1 NY ed.). Retrieved 2011-03-21.</ref>
==Final National History==
{{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} is the final text in the ''Six National Histories'' series. In 936, a national history bureau (撰国史所) was established to maintain the existing national histories as well as to continue with their compilation. A new text, {{lang|ja-latn|[[Shinkokushi]]}}, was begun. However, it remained in draft form and was never completed. The declining power of the [[Ritsuryō]] institution is cited as a cause.
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''; valuable for reconstructing incomplete sections of {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.
